evolution_table.what_changed: Spatial correction vs FitSkin cheek sampling

evolution_table.stage: Affine + cheek + skin + FitSkin lightness (same trials)
evolution_table.what_changed: Pilot reporting calibration; 2 DOF L* gains (P1=1.053, P2=1.063)
evolution_table.note: Re-run with tier3_affine; supersedes lstsq 3x3 FitSkin row (median 3.25)

evolution_table.stage: lstsq 3x3 + cheek + skin + FitSkin lightness (same trials)
evolution_table.what_changed: Tier-2 stack + reporting calibration (lstsq matrix only)
Earlier run (lstsq 3x3 + FitSkin; cheek/skin flags evolved since)
evolution_table.note: Superseded by lstsq+FitSkin 2.88 and affine+FitSkin 2.47 reruns

paper_statements.chart_free_agreement: Primary agreement metric: affine + cheek + skin, median ΔE00 = 3.50 (chart-free, no FitSkin in the fit).
paper_statements.reporting_calibration: Same-trial FitSkin lightness alignment on that stack: median 2.47 (not independent validation). This improves the earlier 3.25 pilot because the underlying stack now includes cheek ROI, skin exposure, and affine calibration—not only the lightness gain.
paper_statements.chart_free_vs_reporting: Chart-free affine (3.50) remains ~1.0 ΔE00 above scanner-aligned reporting (2.47) on the same trials—expected, since 2.47 uses FitSkin L* medians to set two participant gains.
